pi_items.source: Derating calculation: 89 amps (three-phase base) × 80% grouping/bundling derating factor = 71.2 amps ≈ 70 amps. Confirmed via Derating Factors of Cables research and cable industry standards.

spwales.com Cable Size & Current Rating Chart shows single-phase rating of 101 amps for 25 mm² copper cable. The expected_max of 100 is slightly conservative but within reasonable bounds as a rounded safety margin.

spwales.com Cable Size & Current Rating Chart explicitly states three-phase rating for 25 mm² copper cable with PVC insulation is 89 amps. Confirmed by multiple sources including BS 7671 Table 4D2A standards and IEC 60364-5-52 references.
Task setup scripts and export_result.sh confirm the file path. Verifier.py checks for this file existence at the specified location.

privileged_info_summary: The cable ampacity metadata is verified against real electrical engineering standards (IEC 60364-5-52, BS 7671). For a 25 mm² copper cable with PVC insulation in three-phase configuration (3 loaded cores) at 30°C ambient temperature: the target ampacity value of 89 amps matches the three-phase rating in standard ampacity tables. The expected minimum of 70 amps represents the base value with typical derating factors applied (approximately 80% derating for grouped/bundled cables). The expected maximum of 100 amps is a conservative rounded limit, slightly below the single-phase rating of 101 amps. The data is based on the Electrical Calculations Android app which implements real electrical standards, not synthetic values. The task uses standardized installations (free air, perforated tray, methods E/F) which are defined in IEC standards.
